surface_verification.justification: DIAGNOSTIC, not a fix: a runtime renderer override (terminal-renderer.ts) read by xtermAddons.ts; default behavior unchanged (WebGL). The override-resolution logic is fully unit-tested (terminal-renderer.test.ts). The ONLY purpose of this iterate is to let the user A/B the WebGL vs DOM renderer on their REAL GPU to confirm/refute that WebGL is the root cause of the persistent smear -- a determination that is requires-physical-device by definition (headless Chromium WebGL is not representative, and the smear is a visual judgment). No server/API/WS/contract surface touched. The console.info('renderer=...') line gives the user direct confirmation which arm is live.

degraded.reason: requires-physical-device + requires-manual-visual-judgment (real-GPU smear comparison); the user runs the A/B after rebuild

behaviors.behavior: AC-1a: renderer resolves to webgl by default (no query, no storage, null/empty inputs)
AC-1b: renderer resolves to dom when the localStorage value or the ?terminalRenderer= query is dom
behaviors.evidence: terminal-renderer.test.ts 'returns dom when storage value is dom' + 'returns dom when the query opts in'

behaviors.behavior: AC-1c: the query overrides persisted storage in BOTH directions (query=webgl forces default back; query=dom wins)
behaviors.evidence: terminal-renderer.test.ts 'lets the query override persisted storage in BOTH directions'

behaviors.behavior: AC-1d: robustness -- case/whitespace-insensitive, any unknown value -> webgl, malformed search never throws
behaviors.evidence: terminal-renderer.test.ts 'is case- and whitespace-insensitive' + 'treats any unknown value as the webgl default' + 'never throws on a malformed search string'

behaviors.behavior: AC-1e: getTerminalRendererOverride reads localStorage and is SSR/test-safe (webgl default when window absent / storage throws)
behaviors.evidence: terminal-renderer.test.ts 'defaults to webgl with nothing set' + 'reads the dom override from localStorage'

behaviors.behavior: AC-2: switching the embedded terminal to the DOM renderer eliminates the smear across active/idle/replay on a real GPU (the A/B verdict that confirms/refutes WebGL as root cause)
behaviors.reason: the smear is a GPU/compositor-specific visual artifact; headless WebGL is not representative and the comparison is a manual visual judgment. This A/B IS the deliverable -- the user runs it after rebuild.
